enforced.mr_name: RNEXT '=' alias equivalence with explicit RNAME
enforced.oracle: Toggling RNEXT between '=' and the explicit RNAME value preserves the alignment semantics; the record is semantically identical.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: FLAG 0x4 unmapped bit set on a mapped record with real RNAME and POS
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
An unmapped segment without coordinate has a `*' at this field. However, an unmapped segment may also have an ordinary coordinate such that it can be placed at a desired position after sorting.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR query-consuming length mismatch with SEQ length
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired-end reads
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.oracle: Uppercasing SEQ field must not change the parsed content; round-trip through parser must yield identical canonical content since the spec states no assumptions can be made on letter cases.

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads preserves query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all public query methods P on parsed records, where T clears mate-context bits (0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) when FLAG bit 0x1 (multiple segments) is unset, since those bits have no defined meaning for single-segment templates

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle optional tag fields on alignment records
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment line, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation (same parsed tags, same values) as the original file.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @HD header line
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within the @HD line, the SUT must produce identical parsed header metadata (VN, SO, GO, SS values unchanged) as the original file.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote is from a chunk with distance 0.4185 (above threshold), but the rule is well-established in SAMv1 §1.3

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @SQ header lines
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ line, the SUT must produce identical parsed reference sequence metadata (SN, LN, etc. values unchanged) as the original file.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote is from a chunk with distance 0.4185 (above threshold), but the rule is well-established in SAMv1 §1.3

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @RG header lines
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @RG line, the SUT must produce identical parsed read-group metadata (ID, LB, SM, etc. values unchanged) as the original file.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote is from a chunk with distance 0.4185 (above threshold), but the rule is well-established in SAMv1 §1.3

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle TAG:VALUE pairs inside @PG header lines
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @PG line, the SUT must produce identical parsed program record metadata (ID, PN, VN, PP, etc. values unchanged) as the original file.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote is from a chunk with distance 0.4185 (above threshold), but the rule is well-established in SAMv1 §1.3

quarantine.mr_name: Shuffle @CO comment line order in header
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines, the SUT must produce identical parsed header content (all @CO lines present with same text) as the original file.

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE'

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format requirement, implying they are free-text comments with no ordering semantics, but the spec does not explicitly state 'order is not significant' for @CO lines

quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ lines in the header, the SUT must produce identical parsed reference sequence dictionary (same set of @SQ entries with same SN/LN values) as the original file, though coordinate-sorted files may have different sort order implications.

evidence.quote: For coordinate sort, the major sort key is the RNAME field, with order defined by the order of @SQ lines in the header.

ambiguity_flags: @SQ order defines coordinate sort order per SAMv1 §1.3, so reordering @SQ lines changes the semantics for coordinate-sorted files; this MR is only valid for unsorted or queryname-sorted files where @SQ order is not semantically meaningful

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR split/merge of adjacent identical operations preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: After splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations, the total query-consumed length and reference-consumed length remain unchanged, and the alignment semantics are preserved.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY severity — 'should' not 'must', so parsers may accept adjacent identical ops

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length operation removal preserves alignment
quarantine.oracle: Removing zero-length CIGAR operations and merging newly-adjacent identical ops preserves the alignment's query and reference coordinates.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.mr_name: Hard/soft clipping representation equivalence in CIGAR
quarantine.oracle: Converting between hard-clipping (H) and soft-clipping (S) representations while synchronizing SEQ/QUAL preserves the biological alignment semantics.

evidence.quote: H can only be present as the first and/or last operation. S may only have H operations between them and the ends of the CIGAR string.

ambiguity_flags: SEQ content differs between H and S representations (H drops bases, S pads dummy bases), so round-trip equivalence requires careful SEQ/QUAL synchronization

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR match operator canonicalization (M ↔ =/X) with MD tag
quarantine.oracle: Rewriting M (match/mismatch) operators into = (match) and X (mismatch) operators using the MD tag, with NM tag recomputed, preserves the alignment semantics.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: Requires MD tag to be present and correctly formatted; NM tag must be recomputed after the transform

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag type character replaced with illegal character
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.
As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The spec text in the retrieved chunks does not explicitly enumerate the allowed type characters (AifZHB); the rule is defined in SAMtags §2.1 which was not retrieved below threshold.

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with synchronized @SQ LN preserves coordinate validity
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing the corresponding @SQ LN by +shift, every alignment record must still satisfy POS + sum(lengths of M=XDN CIGAR operations) <= LN for its reference sequence. The round-trip through the SUT must produce identical POS and CIGAR values, confirming that the 1-based coordinate system is correctly interpreted relative to the reference sequence length.

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] [1-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[3,7]$. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says POS+ref_consume exceeding LN 'should' make the alignment unmapped (ADVISORY, not CRITICAL). Circular references are an exception. The transform's bound-check no-ops when the shift would cause an overflow, so the MR only tests the valid range.

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped read MAPQ normalization preserves coordinate semantics
quarantine.oracle: After normalizing MAPQ to 255 for unmapped reads (FLAG 0x4), the SUT must produce identical output for all other fields (RNAME, POS, CIGAR, SEQ, QUAL) as the original. The coordinate fields (RNAME, POS) of unmapped reads are left untouched by the transform, and a conformant parser must not change them.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. ... PNEXT: 1-based Position of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. Set as 0 when the information is unavailable.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4 says 'no assumptions can be made' about RNAME/POS/CIGAR for unmapped reads, so the transform leaves them untouched. The MAPQ=255 normalization is well-defined.
